content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 9 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_009_page_001.jpg

1. For concerning the ministry to the holy [ones], it is unnecessary for me to write to you.

2. For I know the readiness of you, which I boast on behalf of you to Makedonians, that Achaia has been prepared since last year, and the zeal from you stirred up the majority.

3. And I sent the brothers, that the boasting of us on behalf of you may not be made empty in this matter, that just as I said, you may be prepared,

4. lest if Makedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we — not to say you — be put to shame in this confidence.

5. Therefore I considered [it] necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange beforehand the blessing of you previously promised, that this may be ready as a blessing and not as greediness.

6. And this: the [one] sowing sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the [one] sowing upon blessings will also reap upon blessings.

7. Each [one] just as he has purposed in the heart, not out of grief or out of necessity, for God loves a cheerful giver.

8. And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that in everything always having all sufficiency, you may abound to every good work,

9. just as it has been written, "He scattered, he gave to the poor, the righteousness of him remains to the age."

10. And the [one] supplying seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply the seed of you and will increase the fruits of the righteousness of you.

11. In everything being enriched to all generosity, which through us produces thanksgiving to God.

12. Because the ministry of this service is not only filling up the needs of the holy [ones], but also overflowing through many thanksgivings to God,

13. through the proof of this ministry glorifying God for the obedience of the confession of you to the good news of the Christos and for the generosity of the fellowship to them and to all,

14. and in the prayer of them on behalf of you, longing for you because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.

15. Thanks [be] to God for his indescribable gift.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.6 — "sowing upon blessings":** ἐπ᾽ εὐλογίαις — literally "upon blessings," an idiom meaning "bountifully/generously." The agricultural metaphor (sowing/reaping) is proverbial (cf. Prov 11:24-25; Gal 6:7-8).

2. **v.7 — "God loves a cheerful giver":** ἱλαρὸν δότην ἀγαπᾷ ὁ θεός — citing Proverbs 22:8a LXX. ἱλαρός means "cheerful/glad" (English "hilarious" derives from it).

3. **v.9 — Psalm 112:9 (LXX 111:9):** The righteous person who gives to the poor — Paul applies this psalm to the Corinthians' giving.

4. **v.10 — Isaiah 55:10:** Paul alludes to the agricultural provision of God who supplies seed and bread. The promise: God supplies the seed for sowing and multiplies the harvest.

5. **v.15 — "indescribable gift":** ἀνεκδιήγητος — a rare word meaning "beyond telling/indescribable." The "gift" is probably Christ himself, though the immediate context is the collection.

— EMET, AI Translator
